title: Visa Q1 Fiscal 2026 Earnings Highlights
text: Visa reported strong fiscal first quarter 2026 results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Net revenue was $10.9 billion, up 15% year over year, or 13% on a constant-dollar basis. GAAP net income was $5.9 billion, up 14%, and GAAP diluted EPS was $3.03, up 17%. Non-GAAP net income was $6.1 billion and non-GAAP EPS was $3.17, up 15%. Business drivers were also strong: payments volume increased 8% in constant dollars, total cross-border volume rose 12%, cross-border volume excluding intra-Europe rose 11%, and processed transactions increased 9%. Visa also returned $5.1 billion to shareholders through repurchases and dividends. This is useful evidence for a stock explanation because it shows broad-based payment growth, resilient consumer spending, cross-border momentum, and strong profitability. Risks to mention include litigation provisions, regulation of payments fees, and the possibility that weaker consumer spending could slow payments volume.
